questions.question_text: How many total clock hours of pre-license education must a Washington broker candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Washington requires 90 clock hours of pre-license education for broker candidates: a 60-hour Fundamentals course and a 30-hour Practices course. The Fundamentals course includes 3 hours of Fair Housing content.

questions.question_text: A broker candidate has completed the 60-hour Fundamentals course. What additional pre-license course is required?
questions.explanation: After completing the 60-hour Fundamentals course, broker candidates must complete the 30-hour Practices course to fulfill the 90-hour pre-license education requirement.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed pre-license education 25 months ago but has not yet taken the exam. What is the status of her education?
questions.explanation: Pre-license education is valid for 2 years (24 months). Since the candidate waited 25 months, her education has expired and she must retake the courses before she can sit for the exam.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a broker license in Washington?
questions.explanation: Washington requires broker candidates to be at least 18 years old and have a high school diploma or equivalent. These are the basic eligibility requirements before completing pre-license education.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the final licensing exam in March. By when must she apply for her broker license?
questions.explanation: Washington requires candidates to apply for their broker license within 1 year of passing the final exam. If they wait longer, they must retake the exam before applying.

questions.question_text: How many years of full-time broker experience are required to qualify for a managing broker license?
questions.explanation: A managing broker candidate must have 3 years of full-time experience as an active broker within the past 5 years. This ensures candidates have recent, substantial practical experience before supervising others.

questions.question_text: A broker practiced full-time for 3 years but has been inactive for 6 years. Can she qualify for a managing broker license?
questions.correct_answer: No, it must be within the past 5 years
distractors: Yes, with a DOL waiver request
Yes, after completing a refresher course
questions.explanation: The 3 years of full-time experience must fall within the past 5 years. Since the broker's experience ended 6 years ago, it falls outside the 5-year window and she does not currently qualify.

questions.question_text: How many additional education hours must a broker complete to qualify for a managing broker license?
questions.explanation: Managing broker candidates must complete 90 additional hours: 30 hours of brokerage management, 30 hours of business management, and 30 hours of advanced real estate law.

questions.question_text: Which three 30-hour courses make up the managing broker education requirement?
distractors: Property mgmt, leasing, and land use
questions.explanation: The 90 additional hours for managing broker candidates consist of three 30-hour courses: brokerage management, business management, and advanced real estate law. These topics prepare candidates for supervisory responsibilities.

questions.question_text: What type of background check is required for managing broker applicants in Washington?
questions.explanation: Managing broker applicants must undergo a fingerprint-based background check. This is processed through the Washington State Patrol and FBI to ensure applicants meet character and fitness standards.

questions.question_text: A broker applicant has a prior felony conviction on their record. What effect does this have on their application?
questions.correct_answer: The DOL reviews it on a case-by-case basis
questions.explanation: A criminal history does not automatically disqualify an applicant. The DOL reviews the nature, severity, and recency of the offense on a case-by-case basis when evaluating character and fitness for licensure.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the licensing exam. What must she do before she can legally practice real estate?
questions.explanation: After passing the exam, a candidate must apply for a license and affiliate with a licensed brokerage firm before practicing real estate. A broker cannot practice independently without firm affiliation in Washington.

questions.question_text: A broker's fingerprint results are submitted to which two agencies during the application process?
questions.explanation: Fingerprints submitted through IdentoGO are processed by both the Washington State Patrol (WSP) for state records and the FBI for federal criminal history. Both checks must clear before a license is issued.

questions.question_text: A brokerage firm wants to open a second location. What must the firm obtain from the DOL?
distractors: Permission from the Real Estate Commission
questions.explanation: Each branch office location requires its own branch office license from the DOL. Additionally, a managing broker must be assigned to supervise each branch location to ensure adequate oversight.

questions.question_text: A candidate passed the broker exam 14 months ago but never applied for a license. What must she do now?
questions.explanation: Candidates must apply for their license within 1 year of passing the final exam. Since 14 months have passed, this candidate's exam results have expired and she must retake the exam before applying.
